_meta.description: Product roadmap source of truth for Maintenance Agent. Edit custom_items[] to add user stories and tasks. Feature statuses are auto-detected from the codebase.

project.name: Maintenance Agent
project.subtitle: Full-stack property maintenance platform — Swiss market

phases.name: Foundation Hardening
phases.goal: Clear the critical path blockers before adding any new features. Three items in your backlog directly block monetizable work.

phases.name: Cashflow Layer
phases.goal: Build the financial visibility layer for building owners — cash in, cash out, per-building balance — without crossing into accounting. Anchored on BuildingFinancialSnapshot.

phases.name: Private Landlord SaaS
phases.goal: The current product is built for professional managers. This phase creates a simplified entry point for private individuals owning 1–20 properties — same backend, different UX skin and onboarding.

phases.name: AI Triage Upgrade
phases.goal: Extend the existing triage service from classify-and-route to resolve-before-dispatch. The infrastructure is there. The intelligence layer is not.

phases.name: NOI Intelligence
phases.goal: Connect cashflow + maintenance analytics + vacancy data into a per-building P&L proxy. Explicitly not accounting. The output is decision intelligence, not a financial statement.

phases.name: ESG Layer
phases.goal: Energy tracking, cantonal compliance deadlines, retrofit ROI modeling. New vertical targeting institutional owners.

features.title: MT940 Bank Statement Import
features.description: Accept MT940/CSV bank exports from all major Swiss banks. Auto-match incoming payments to open rent demands by amount + reference. Flag unmatched.

features.title: BuildingFinancialSnapshot Engine
features.description: The model exists. Build the service that populates it: monthly snapshot of income, expenses, vacancy loss, net balance. Central data structure for P4.

features.title: Arrears Management + Automated Reminders
features.description: Escalating reminder workflow: friendly nudge (D+3) → formal notice (D+14) → Betreibungshinweis template (D+30). All emails sent via EmailOutbox.

features.title: Nebenkostenabrechnung Generator
features.description: Annual utility charge statement generation. Input: expense invoices tagged as Nebenkosten over 12 months. Output: PDF per tenant showing their share. OR Art. 257a/b compliant.

features.title: Owner Cashflow Dashboard
features.description: Extend /owner/financials into a real cashflow dashboard. Per-building monthly view: income bar, expense bar, net balance line. Maintenance events shown as expense spikes.

features.title: Self-Service Onboarding Wizard
features.description: Private landlord operational in under 15 minutes: add building → add unit → upload/create lease → set rent → generate first QR-invoice. Multi-language from day one.

features.title: Owner-as-Manager Unified Mode
features.description: OrgConfig flag that collapses Manager and Owner personas into a single simplified interface for private landlords who manage their own properties.

features.title: Multilingual Tenant Portal (FR/DE/IT)
features.description: All tenant-facing pages serve FR/DE/IT based on tenant language preference. Without this, the platform is only usable in monolingual cantons.

features.title: Canton-Aware Initial Rent Notice (Formular B)
features.description: Generate the correct canton-specific initial rent notification form from lease data, pre-filled, ready to sign. First tangible proof of legal layer value.

features.title: PWA / Mobile-Optimized Tenant Portal
features.description: Wrap tenant portal as a PWA — installable on iOS/Android, push notifications, offline document access. Replaces phone calls for a private landlord’s tenant.

features.title: Document Vault
features.description: Structured document storage — lease PDFs, signed addenda, Nebenkosten statements, correspondence — linked to tenant and property, searchable, exportable.

features.title: Photo + Video Evidence on Intake
features.description: Tenant attaches photos/video when submitting a request. Forwarded to contractor before dispatch — eliminates ‘I need to assess first’ callout.

features.title: LLM Classification + Urgency Scoring
features.description: Replace/augment rule-based triage with LLM-based classification. Input: free-text + photos. Output: category, urgency score (1–5), suggested contractor type, legal obligation flag.

features.title: Guided Troubleshooting Flows
features.description: When tenant submits a request, present structured Q&A flow to resolve at intake without dispatch. Target: resolve ~20% without sending a contractor.

features.title: Maintenance Analytics Dashboard
features.description: Requests by category/building, avg time-to-resolution, self-resolution rate, contractor performance, cost-per-category. Resolves the reports.js backlog item.

features.title: Maintenance → Cost Attribution Engine
features.description: Link every closed maintenance request to its total cost. Tag as capex or opex. Maintenance events appear as tagged expense events on building cashflow timeline.

features.title: Vacancy Impact Quantification
features.description: Calculate and display vacancy cost as a negative cash event. Link to reason where known. Makes the cost of slow re-letting viscerally clear.

features.title: NOI Proxy Dashboard
features.description: Per-building: gross income, total expenses (with maintenance breakdown), vacancy loss, net operating income proxy. Clearly labelled indicative. Year-on-year comparison.

features.title: Proactive Asset Health Signals
features.description: Use AssetIntervention + DepreciationStandard to surface forward-looking signals: aging assets, recommended replacement budget, projected future expense.

features.title: Treuhänder / Accountant Export Package
features.description: One-click annual summary export for accountant: all income, expense invoices, Nebenkosten statements, lease periods, vacancy periods. Abacus CSV format.

features.title: bLink Open Banking Feed
features.description: Replace MT940 manual import with live bank feed via SIX bLink. Automatic daily reconciliation. Build only after MT940 is proven and trusted.

custom_items.title: Fixed Asset Register + Depreciation
custom_items.description: Track property acquisition cost and post annual depreciation (Swiss norm: 1–2% p.a. linear for residential buildings, land not depreciated). Required to show property book value on the Balance Sheet and depreciation expense (4700) on the Income Statement. Without this, the Balance Sheet asset side is significantly understated.
acceptance_criteria: Building model gains acquisitionCostCents (Int), acquisitionDate (DateTime), landValueCents (Int) fields via migration
Annual cron or manual trigger posts Dr 4700 Depreciation / Cr accumulated depreciation contra-asset at configured rate
/admin-inventory/buildings/:id has a "Asset Values" panel to enter acquisition cost, land split, depreciation rate
Balance Sheet includes building at net book value (cost − accumulated depreciation)
npx tsc --noEmit — zero errors
required_tests: Verify feature works as described in acceptance criteria
No regressions in existing test suite

1. npx tsc --noEmit — zero TypeScript errors
2. npm test — all tests pass
3. npm run blueprint — architecture docs sync

PDF export matches Formular Liegenschaftsunterhalt structure (rental income, deductions, net result)
Accessible at /owner/tax-reports and /manager/buildings/:id/tax-report

grey_zone: Email delivery (outbox exists, no SMTP provider wired)

we_dont_own: Formal accounting (VAT, balance sheet, P&L — belongs in Abacus/Sage)
LLM triage (rule-based only, no OpenAI integration)
Vision-based damage classification (image upload exists via MaintenanceAttachment, no AI analysis)
GEAK energy ratings (no Swiss government API integration)

intake_items.story_success_outcome: http://localhost:3000/manager/legal?tab=category_mappings: this was originally designed to map some maintenance requests topics to some broader legal areas. The rent reduction rules systematically return "no rules" which is not possible. This means that the reading of legal documents and interpretation in http://localhost:3000/manager/legal?tab=sources is done superficially or not at all. As http://localhost:3000/manager/legal?tab=category_mappings is used for the determination of legal obligation of an owner to address a tenant request, this has rippled effects downstream. Not sure where the problem is but we need to figure out a way to increase the confidence that the scanning of legal sources is done with meticulous attention.

intake_items.raw_text: Fix 28 pre-existing server-spawn test timeouts — multiple test suites (auth.manager-gates, tenantSession, ia, contracts, rentalContracts, rentEstimation) have server-spawn-based integration tests that intermittently timeout under Jest load. Root cause: each test suite spawns a full server process via tsx, and under parallel Jest workers + CI resource constraints, startup can exceed 15-30s timeouts. Possible fixes: shared server instance across suites, in-process testing with supertest, or increase CI resources.

intake_items.raw_text:
EPIC — Swiss Property Tax Statement Journey (Owner / Manager Workspace)

Proposed date: 2026-03-22
Status: Draft
Priority: High
Type: PRODUCT / FINANCIAL / WORKFLOW
Suggested ID: EPIC-TAX-STATEMENT-001

Summary

Add a structured Tax Statement Journey to the platform so a Swiss property owner or property manager can compile the data needed for annual tax declaration of real estate holdings directly from the tool.

The journey should support:

property wealth reporting (tax value / fiscal value),
property income reporting (actual rent or owner-occupancy imputed rental value),
deduction reporting (mortgage interest, maintenance, eligible costs),
and source reconciliation so users can see exactly where each reported number came from.

This epic does not attempt to fully automate Swiss tax filing submission. It creates a tax-ready statement and reconciliation workflow that helps users prepare, verify, and export the numbers needed for their declaration with strong auditability and jurisdiction-aware rules.

Why this epic now

The product already contains a growing set of real-estate operational data:

leases,
buildings,
units,
owners,
invoices,
charges,
expenses,
financial pages,
legal/rules infrastructure,
and document/PDF generation patterns.

That makes the platform close to being able to support a high-value owner workflow:
turning operational property data into a tax-preparation artifact.

Today, users likely need to assemble tax inputs manually from:

leases,
bank statements,
maintenance invoices,
valuation notices,
rent rolls,
and ad hoc spreadsheets.

This epic closes that gap by introducing a guided workflow that:

identifies the relevant tax treatment per property,
computes or imports the required values,
reconciles them against source documents,
and generates a tax statement package for owner review.

This fits the product direction because it extends existing financial/property data into an owner-facing annual workflow without requiring a full accounting or tax filing product.

Problem statement

Swiss property owners generally need a reliable annual summary for tax reporting that can distinguish between:

wealth-side reporting of property value,
income-side reporting of rent or imputed rental value,
deductible financing costs such as mortgage interest,
maintenance and eligible property costs,
and special treatment such as foreign property rate effects or canton-specific valuation rules.

The system currently has pieces of the data, but no end-to-end journey that answers:

Which numbers belong in the tax declaration?
Which values are imported versus computed?
Which source document supports each line?
Which items are deductible vs informational only?
How does owner-occupied property differ from rented property?
How can the user export a clean, reviewable statement?

Without this journey, users must manually reconcile scattered property data, which is slow, error-prone, and low-confidence.

User personas
Primary
Owner — wants a tax-ready annual property statement
Property Manager — prepares the statement package on behalf of the owner
Secondary
Accountant / fiduciary — reviews exported statement and source mapping
Internal staff — uses the system to classify and validate costs
Product goals
G1. Tax-prep journey, not raw data dump

Guide the user through a clear annual workflow rather than exposing disconnected reports.

G2. Rule-aware treatment of Swiss property tax inputs

Support the core distinctions discussed earlier:

property tax value / fiscal value,
owner-occupied imputed rental value,
actual rental income,
mortgage interest,
maintenance deductions,
property classification / usage,
optional foreign property handling.
G3. Source-backed numbers

Every figure shown in the tax statement must be marked as:

imported,
computed,
manually entered,
or unresolved.
G4. Reconciliation confidence

Users should be able to compare statement lines to source evidence, similar in spirit to a salary certificate workflow, even if no single Swiss-standard property certificate exists.

G5. Configurable jurisdiction model

Swiss treatment varies by canton and by property situation. The system must be configurable and explicit about assumptions rather than hard-coding fragile tax logic.

Non-goals

This epic does not:

submit tax returns to cantonal authorities,
provide certified legal or tax advice,
replace accountants/fiduciaries,
guarantee full canton-by-canton legal completeness on day 1,
or attempt full mortgage/banking integration in the first slice.

It produces a tax-preparation statement and evidence trail, not a filing system.

Core user journey
1. Start annual tax statement

From the Owner or Manager workspace, the user starts a new Tax Statement for a given tax year.

They choose:

tax year,
owner,
one or more properties,
declaration context (owner-prepared or manager-prepared),
canton / tax jurisdiction context.
2. Classify each property for tax treatment

For each property, the journey asks for or infers:

ownership type,
property location,
whether the property is:
owner-occupied,
fully rented,
partially rented,
vacant,
foreign property.

This classification determines which lines are required.

3. Gather statement inputs

The journey pulls from platform data where available and requests missing data where needed.

Imported or existing platform data
leases and rent rolls,
invoices and expense records,
building/unit ownership links,
charges and related property income structures,
known property metadata.
Manually entered or attached external data
official tax value / fiscal value,
imputed rental value when authority-provided,
mortgage balances and annual interest,
valuation notices,
bank mortgage attestations,
off-platform maintenance costs,
foreign property values where relevant.
4. Compute statement lines

The system computes draft values using rule-aware logic.

Wealth section
property fiscal/tax value
related debt reference where included in the statement package
Income section

Depending on property classification:

actual rental income, or
imputed rental value for owner-occupied property
Deduction section
mortgage interest
maintenance costs
eligible renovation / upkeep costs
excluded or ambiguous expenses flagged for review
5. Reconcile numbers to evidence

For every tax statement line, the UI shows:

value,
source type,
source record(s),
rule used,
confidence / review status,
unresolved issues.

This creates a “property tax statement workbook” equivalent to the confidence people get from a salary certificate, even though no single standard Swiss document exists for property reporting.

6. Review exceptions

The system flags issues such as:

missing tax value,
missing mortgage interest attestation,
occupancy classification conflict,
maintenance cost not fully categorized,
property with both owner-use and rental use,
foreign property missing rate-only handling flag,
canton rule set incomplete or unconfigured.
7. Generate export package

Export a tax-prep package containing:

tax statement summary by property,
aggregated owner summary,
reconciliation appendix,
source checklist,
optional PDF and CSV export.
Functional scope
In scope
Tax statement entity and workflow

A new annual statement workflow for owners/managers.

Property tax data model

A normalized place to store:

tax year,
property classification,
tax values,
income treatment,
deductions,
source mappings,
review status.
Rule-aware line generation

Line-item logic for:

wealth value,
rental income,
imputed rental value,
mortgage interest,
maintenance deductions,
manual adjustments.
Evidence and reconciliation layer

Each statement line maps to supporting records or uploaded attestations.

Export layer

Generate statement summary and supporting package.

Out of scope for first version
direct e-filing integrations,
automated bank ingestion,
OCR-first automation for all third-party tax documents,
canton-perfect legal coverage across all Switzerland,
property sale / capital gains tax workflows,
depreciation-based accounting treatment as a tax filing substitute.
Tax rules to incorporate in v1

These should be modeled as configurable tax treatment rules, not brittle hard-coded assumptions.

R1. Property wealth reporting

Each property may require a declared tax/fiscal value for wealth reporting.

System behavior:

support official imported value,
support manual entry with source attachment,
support jurisdiction metadata,
do not silently derive a fiscal value unless rule configuration explicitly allows it.
R2. Income reporting by usage

A property is taxed differently depending on use.

Supported classifications:

owner-occupied,
rented,
mixed-use,
vacant,
foreign property.

System behavior:

owner-occupied → imputed rental path
rented → actual rent path
mixed-use → split or manual review path
foreign property → marked for jurisdiction-specific handling
R3. Imputed rental value

For owner-occupied property, a statement may require imputed rental value.

System behavior:

support authority-provided amount,
support configured calculation method where enabled,
always display calculation basis and assumption source,
flag for review if the canton rule set is missing or ambiguous.
R4. Actual rental income

For rented properties, statement includes actual rental income.

System behavior:

derive from lease/rent data where available,
distinguish rent from non-taxable pass-through reimbursements where configured,
support manual override with explanation.
R5. Mortgage interest deduction

Mortgage interest may be deductible.

System behavior:

support annual imported/manual bank attestation entry,
separate principal from interest,
attach the attestation to the statement line,
do not infer interest from outstanding balance alone.
R6. Maintenance cost deduction

Maintenance and upkeep may be deductible, subject to classification.

System behavior:

map invoices/expenses into tax categories:
maintenance,
capital / improvement,
insurance/admin,
energy/other eligible category,
unclassified.
allow actual-cost method,
allow flat-rate method if configured,
never double-count both actual and flat-rate deduction.
R7. Foreign property informational handling

Foreign property may affect the tax rate even when not directly taxed in the same way.

System behavior:

store separately,
label as rate-influencing / jurisdiction-dependent,
require explicit configuration and review.
R8. No single standard Swiss “property salary certificate”

There is no universal salary-certificate equivalent for property reporting.

System behavior:

generate an internal Tax Statement Reconciliation Report that plays the same role:
statement line,
source document,
source field,
computed value,
declaration target field,
review status.
Proposed new product objects

These are conceptual and may map to Prisma models, supporting tables, or existing entities.

1. TaxStatement

Annual container for an owner/tax year.

Suggested fields:

id
orgId
ownerId
taxYear
jurisdictionType
canton
status (DRAFT, IN_REVIEW, READY, EXPORTED, ARCHIVED)
preparedByUserId
reviewedByUserId
timestamps
2. TaxStatementProperty

Links a statement to a building/property and stores tax treatment.

Suggested fields:

taxStatementId
buildingId or canonical property reference
usageType
isForeignProperty
countryCode
canton
officialTaxValue
officialImputedRentalValue
notes
reviewStatus
3. TaxStatementLine

Normalized line items.

Suggested fields:

taxStatementPropertyId
lineType
PROPERTY_TAX_VALUE
RENTAL_INCOME
IMPUTED_RENTAL_VALUE
MORTGAGE_INTEREST
MAINTENANCE_DEDUCTION
MANUAL_ADJUSTMENT
RATE_ONLY_FOREIGN_PROPERTY
amount
currency
sourceMode (IMPORTED, COMPUTED, MANUAL)
ruleKey
confidence
isOverride
reviewStatus
4. TaxStatementEvidence

Source linkage.

Suggested fields:

taxStatementLineId
sourceEntityType
sourceEntityId
sourceDocumentId
sourceLabel
sourceField
notes
5. TaxRuleProfile

Configuration layer by canton/jurisdiction.

Suggested fields:

jurisdictionCode
taxYear
supportsImputedRental
imputedRentalMethod
maintenanceDeductionMethod
requiresOfficialTaxValue
supportsForeignRateOnlyHandling
isActive
UX scope
Owner / Manager entry points
Owner portal

Add a new navigation item or finance sub-page:

/owner/tax-statements
Manager portal

Add a corresponding workspace:

/manager/financials/tax-statements
or a finance tab under the existing finance area.

Use existing manager layout guardrails:

hub page if multiple tabs are needed,
detail page structure for statement review,
Panel sections for summary / sources / exceptions / export.
Suggested screen flow
A. Statement list page

Shows:

tax year
owner
number of properties
status
unresolved issues
last export date
B. Statement builder wizard

Steps:

Select year + owner
Select properties
Classify usage
Enter/import official values
Review income and deductions
Resolve issues
Generate package
C. Statement detail page

Sections:

Summary
Properties
Income lines
Deduction lines
Reconciliation
Exceptions
Exports
D. Reconciliation view

Most important UX in the epic.

A table-like or grouped view:

declaration field / statement line
amount
source
calculation method
evidence attached
confidence
issue badge
Backend architecture fit

This epic should fit the existing backend shape:

routes/ → thin handlers
workflows/ → orchestration for statement creation / recompute / finalize
services/ → tax line generation, deduction classification, validation
repositories/ → canonical access to statements, evidence, property financials
validation/ → zod schemas for manual entries and overrides
Proposed workflow additions
createTaxStatementWorkflow.ts
recomputeTaxStatementWorkflow.ts
finalizeTaxStatementWorkflow.ts
exportTaxStatementWorkflow.ts
Proposed repository additions
taxStatementRepository.ts
possibly financialEvidenceRepository.ts if the evidence layer becomes reusable
Guardrails for this epic

This epic touches financial and semi-regulatory data, so the guardrails should be stricter than average.

TX1: No silent tax assumptions

If a figure depends on jurisdiction-specific logic and the rule profile is missing, the line must be flagged REQUIRES_REVIEW, not silently computed.

TX2: Every computed line needs provenance

Each computed line must store:

rule used,
input values,
calculation basis,
source mode.
TX3: Manual override must remain visible

If a user overrides a computed figure, the original computed value must remain inspectable.

TX4: Imported bank/official values are authoritative when configured

For mortgage interest and official tax values, imported/manual official figures should take precedence over inferred approximations.

TX5: No mixing actual and flat-rate maintenance deduction

If the tax profile supports both methods, the statement must enforce one chosen method per property/year.

TX6: Tax statement output is “tax-prep support,” not certified advice

UI and export must clearly indicate:

prepared from available data,
subject to owner/accountant review,
jurisdiction configuration dependent.
TX7: Canonical include definitions apply

Any DTO that returns tax statement detail must use centralized includes per G9.

TX8: Contract tests required

New endpoints must have contract tests asserting:

line shape,
evidence linkage,
property classification,
totals and status fields.
Proposed implementation slices
Slice 1 — Data model + statement shell

Create the core statement object and basic CRUD/listing.

Deliverables
schema additions for tax statement entities
routes for create/list/get/update
owner/manager list page
statement detail shell
Acceptance criteria
user can create a tax statement for a year/owner
user can attach one or more properties
statement persists status and review metadata
no DTO/include drift
Slice 2 — Property classification + official values capture

Add property usage classification and input of official tax values / imputed rental values.

Deliverables
wizard step or detail form
validation schemas
support for official value entry
optional source attachment linkage
Acceptance criteria
each property must be classified before statement can finalize
owner-occupied and rented properties take different paths
missing required official values block readiness and produce actionable issues
Slice 3 — Income line generation

Generate rental or imputed-rent lines per property.

Deliverables
tax line generator service
lease/rent roll extraction for rental income
imputed rental manual/imported/configured-calculation path
property-level income section UI
Acceptance criteria
rented property generates rental-income line
owner-occupied property generates imputed-rental path
mixed-use property creates split/review issue instead of bad silent output
Slice 4 — Deduction engine for mortgage interest and maintenance

Add deductions with evidence mapping.

Deliverables
mortgage interest input + evidence
expense classification into deductible buckets
actual-vs-flat-rate method selection
statement deduction section
Acceptance criteria
mortgage interest cannot be inferred from balance alone
maintenance deduction source records are inspectable
disallowed or unclassified expenses are visibly excluded or flagged
no double counting across deduction methods
Slice 5 — Reconciliation report

Build the “salary certificate equivalent” inside the product.

Deliverables
reconciliation grid/report
source-to-line mapping
exportable appendix
confidence / issue badges
Acceptance criteria
every material statement line has a visible source path
exported report helps an accountant map numbers into a tax declaration
manual entries are distinguishable from computed/imported lines
Slice 6 — Export package

Generate PDF/CSV package for owner/accountant review.

Deliverables
PDF summary
CSV line export
evidence checklist
optional per-property appendix
Acceptance criteria
user can export statement package
export includes assumptions and unresolved issues
totals in export match UI totals exactly
Future slices
canton-specific rule packs
OCR-assisted import of bank/tax authority attestations
accountant review mode
cross-year carry-forward comparisons
foreign property expanded handling
sale / capital gains / major renovation treatment
Validation checklist

Before merge of any slice:

Backend
schema drift check empty
prisma generate
tsc --noEmit
canonical include constants for tax statement DTOs
contract tests for new tax statement endpoints
Frontend
follows F-UI1/F-UI2 depending on page type
no inline styles
repeated stateful tax UI extracted into shared components if reused
empty states and review banners use global component classes
Financial correctness
totals = sum of constituent lines
override state is preserved
excluded lines are visible, not dropped
source mapping exists for all material statement lines
Risks
R1. Canton specificity

Swiss tax treatment varies. Hard-coding one-size-fits-all logic will create false confidence.

Mitigation: rule profiles + review-required status when incomplete.

R2. Source incompleteness

The platform may not hold all necessary external tax data.

Mitigation: first-class manual entry and evidence attachment, not fake automation.

R3. Expense classification ambiguity

Maintenance vs improvement may not always be derivable from invoice data.

Mitigation: classification workflow + review queue + manual override.

R4. Overlap with finance/reporting

This epic can sprawl into full accounting.

Mitigation: keep scope to annual tax-prep statement generation.

Definition of done

This epic is done when:

An owner or manager can create an annual tax statement in the tool.
Properties can be classified by tax treatment.
The system generates the key wealth, income, and deduction lines.
Every material line has visible provenance.
The user can export a reviewable statement package.
Missing legal/jurisdiction data results in explicit review flags, not silent assumptions.
Contract tests and financial correctness tests protect the new surface.
Suggested epic-history narrative

Delivered a Swiss Property Tax Statement Journey for owners and managers, turning operational real-estate data into a tax-preparation workflow inside the platform. Added annual tax statement entities, property usage classification, line generation for tax value, rental income or imputed rental value, mortgage interest, and maintenance deductions, plus a reconciliation layer that maps each figure to its evidence and calculation rule. The journey does not file taxes directly; instead it produces a jurisdiction-aware, reviewable statement package that gives owners and accountants confidence similar to a salary certificate workflow, without hiding missing data or hard-coding brittle legal assumptions.

intake_items.raw_text: EPIC — Swiss Property Tax Statement Journey (Owner / Manager Workspace) Proposed date: 2026-03-22 Status: Draft Priority: High Type: PRODUCT / FINANCIAL / WORKFLOW Suggested ID: EPIC-TAX-STATEMENT-001 Summary Add a structured Tax Statement Journey to the platform so a Swiss property owner or property manager can compile the data needed for annual tax declaration of real estate holdings directly from the tool. The journey should support: property wealth reporting (tax value / fiscal value), property income reporting (actual rent or owner-occupancy imputed rental value), deduction reporting (mortgage interest, maintenance, eligible costs), and source reconciliation so users can see exactly where each reported number came from. This epic does not attempt to fully automate Swiss tax filing submission. It creates a tax-ready statement and reconciliation workflow that helps users prepare, verify, and export the numbers needed for their declaration with strong auditability and jurisdiction-aware rules. Why this epic now The product already contains a growing set of real-estate operational data: leases, buildings, units, owners, invoices, charges, expenses, financial pages, legal/rules infrastructure, and document/PDF generation patterns. That makes the platform close to being able to support a high-value owner workflow: turning operational property data into a tax-preparation artifact. Today, users likely need to assemble tax inputs manually from: leases, bank statements, maintenance invoices, valuation notices, rent rolls, and ad hoc spreadsheets. This epic closes that gap by introducing a guided workflow that: identifies the relevant tax treatment per property, computes or imports the required values, reconciles them against source documents, and generates a tax statement package for owner review. This fits the product direction because it extends existing financial/property data into an owner-facing annual workflow without requiring a full accounting or tax filing product. Problem statement Swiss property owners generally need a reliable annual summary for tax reporting that can distinguish between: wealth-side reporting of property value, income-side reporting of rent or imputed rental value, deductible financing costs such as mortgage interest, maintenance and eligible property costs, and special treatment such as foreign property rate effects or canton-specific valuation rules. The system currently has pieces of the data, but no end-to-end journey that answers: Which numbers belong in the tax declaration? Which values are imported versus computed? Which source document supports each line? Which items are deductible vs informational only? How does owner-occupied property differ from rented property? How can the user export a clean, reviewable statement? Without this journey, users must manually reconcile scattered property data, which is slow, error-prone, and low-confidence. User personas Primary Owner — wants a tax-ready annual property statement Property Manager — prepares the statement package on behalf of the owner Secondary Accountant / fiduciary — reviews exported statement and source mapping Internal staff — uses the system to classify and validate costs Product goals G1. Tax-prep journey, not raw data dump Guide the user through a clear annual workflow rather than exposing disconnected reports. G2. Rule-aware treatment of Swiss property tax inputs Support the core distinctions discussed earlier: property tax value / fiscal value, owner-occupied imputed rental value, actual rental income, mortgage interest, maintenance deductions, property classification / usage, optional foreign property handling. G3. Source-backed numbers Every figure shown in the tax statement must be marked as: imported, computed, manually entered, or unresolved. G4. Reconciliation confidence Users should be able to compare statement lines to source evidence, similar in spirit to a salary certificate workflow, even if no single Swiss-standard property certificate exists. G5. Configurable jurisdiction model Swiss treatment varies by canton and by property situation. The system must be configurable and explicit about assumptions rather than hard-coding fragile tax logic. Non-goals This epic does not: submit tax returns to cantonal authorities, provide certified legal or tax advice, replace accountants/fiduciaries, guarantee full canton-by-canton legal completeness on day 1, or attempt full mortgage/banking integration in the first slice. It produces a tax-preparation statement and evidence trail, not a filing system. Core user journey 1. Start annual tax statement From the Owner or Manager workspace, the user starts a new Tax Statement for a given tax year. They choose: tax year, owner, one or more properties, declaration context (owner-prepared or manager-prepared), canton / tax jurisdiction context. 2. Classify each property for tax treatment For each property, the journey asks for or infers: ownership type, property location, whether the property is: owner-occupied, fully rented, partially rented, vacant, foreign property. This classification determines which lines are required. 3. Gather statement inputs The journey pulls from platform data where available and requests missing data where needed. Imported or existing platform data leases and rent rolls, invoices and expense records, building/unit ownership links, charges and related property income structures, known property metadata. Manually entered or attached external data official tax value / fiscal value, imputed rental value when authority-provided, mortgage balances and annual interest, valuation notices, bank mortgage attestations, off-platform maintenance costs, foreign property values where relevant. 4. Compute statement lines The system computes draft values using rule-aware logic. Wealth section property fiscal/tax value related debt reference where included in the statement package Income section Depending on property classification: actual rental income, or imputed rental value for owner-occupied property Deduction section mortgage interest maintenance costs eligible renovation / upkeep costs excluded or ambiguous expenses flagged for review 5. Reconcile numbers to evidence For every tax statement line, the UI shows: value, source type, source record(s), rule used, confidence / review status, unresolved issues. This creates a “property tax statement workbook” equivalent to the confidence people get from a salary certificate, even though no single standard Swiss document exists for property reporting. 6. Review exceptions The system flags issues such as: missing tax value, missing mortgage interest attestation, occupancy classification conflict, maintenance cost not fully categorized, property with both owner-use and rental use, foreign property missing rate-only handling flag, canton rule set incomplete or unconfigured. 7. Generate export package Export a tax-prep package containing: tax statement summary by property, aggregated owner summary, reconciliation appendix, source checklist, optional PDF and CSV export. Functional scope In scope Tax statement entity and workflow A new annual statement workflow for owners/managers. Property tax data model A normalized place to store: tax year, property classification, tax values, income treatment, deductions, source mappings, review status. Rule-aware line generation Line-item logic for: wealth value, rental income, imputed rental value, mortgage interest, maintenance deductions, manual adjustments. Evidence and reconciliation layer Each statement line maps to supporting records or uploaded attestations. Export layer Generate statement summary and supporting package. Out of scope for first version direct e-filing integrations, automated bank ingestion, OCR-first automation for all third-party tax documents, canton-perfect legal coverage across all Switzerland, property sale / capital gains tax workflows, depreciation-based accounting treatment as a tax filing substitute. Tax rules to incorporate in v1 These should be modeled as configurable tax treatment rules, not brittle hard-coded assumptions. R1. Property wealth reporting Each property may require a declared tax/fiscal value for wealth reporting. System behavior: support official imported value, support manual entry with source attachment, support jurisdiction metadata, do not silently derive a fiscal value unless rule configuration explicitly allows it. R2. Income reporting by usage A property is taxed differently depending on use. Supported classifications: owner-occupied, rented, mixed-use, vacant, foreign property. System behavior: owner-occupied → imputed rental path rented → actual rent path mixed-use → split or manual review path foreign property → marked for jurisdiction-specific handling R3. Imputed rental value For owner-occupied property, a statement may require imputed rental value. System behavior: support authority-provided amount, support configured calculation method where enabled, always display calculation basis and assumption source, flag for review if the canton rule set is missing or ambiguous. R4. Actual rental income For rented properties, statement includes actual rental income. System behavior: derive from lease/rent data where available, distinguish rent from non-taxable pass-through reimbursements where configured, support manual override with explanation. R5. Mortgage interest deduction Mortgage interest may be deductible. System behavior: support annual imported/manual bank attestation entry, separate principal from interest, attach the attestation to the statement line, do not infer interest from outstanding balance alone. R6. Maintenance cost deduction Maintenance and upkeep may be deductible, subject to classification. System behavior: map invoices/expenses into tax categories: maintenance, capital / improvement, insurance/admin, energy/other eligible category, unclassified. allow actual-cost method, allow flat-rate method if configured, never double-count both actual and flat-rate deduction. R7. Foreign property informational handling Foreign property may affect the tax rate even when not directly taxed in the same way. System behavior: store separately, label as rate-influencing / jurisdiction-dependent, require explicit configuration and review. R8. No single standard Swiss “property salary certificate” There is no universal salary-certificate equivalent for property reporting. System behavior: generate an internal Tax Statement Reconciliation Report that plays the same role: statement line, source document, source field, computed value, declaration target field, review status. Proposed new product objects These are conceptual and may map to Prisma models, supporting tables, or existing entities. 1. TaxStatement Annual container for an owner/tax year. Suggested fields: id orgId ownerId taxYear jurisdictionType canton status (DRAFT, IN_REVIEW, READY, EXPORTED, ARCHIVED) preparedByUserId reviewedByUserId timestamps 2. TaxStatementProperty Links a statement to a building/property and stores tax treatment. Suggested fields: taxStatementId buildingId or canonical property reference usageType isForeignProperty countryCode canton officialTaxValue officialImputedRentalValue notes reviewStatus 3. TaxStatementLine Normalized line items. Suggested fields: taxStatementPropertyId lineType PROPERTY_TAX_VALUE RENTAL_INCOME IMPUTED_RENTAL_VALUE MORTGAGE_INTEREST MAINTENANCE_DEDUCTION MANUAL_ADJUSTMENT RATE_ONLY_FOREIGN_PROPERTY amount currency sourceMode (IMPORTED, COMPUTED, MANUAL) ruleKey confidence isOverride reviewStatus 4. TaxStatementEvidence Source linkage. Suggested fields: taxStatementLineId sourceEntityType sourceEntityId sourceDocumentId sourceLabel sourceField notes 5. TaxRuleProfile Configuration layer by canton/jurisdiction. Suggested fields: jurisdictionCode taxYear supportsImputedRental imputedRentalMethod maintenanceDeductionMethod requiresOfficialTaxValue supportsForeignRateOnlyHandling isActive UX scope Owner / Manager entry points Owner portal Add a new navigation item or finance sub-page: /owner/tax-statements Manager portal Add a corresponding workspace: /manager/financials/tax-statements or a finance tab under the existing finance area. Use existing manager layout guardrails: hub page if multiple tabs are needed, detail page structure for statement review, Panel sections for summary / sources / exceptions / export. Suggested screen flow A. Statement list page Shows: tax year owner number of properties status unresolved issues last export date B. Statement builder wizard Steps: Select year + owner Select properties Classify usage Enter/import official values Review income and deductions Resolve issues Generate package C. Statement detail page Sections: Summary Properties Income lines Deduction lines Reconciliation Exceptions Exports D. Reconciliation view Most important UX in the epic. A table-like or grouped view: declaration field / statement line amount source calculation method evidence attached confidence issue badge Backend architecture fit This epic should fit the existing backend shape: routes/ → thin handlers workflows/ → orchestration for statement creation / recompute / finalize services/ → tax line generation, deduction classification, validation repositories/ → canonical access to statements, evidence, property financials validation/ → zod schemas for manual entries and overrides Proposed workflow additions createTaxStatementWorkflow.ts recomputeTaxStatementWorkflow.ts finalizeTaxStatementWorkflow.ts exportTaxStatementWorkflow.ts Proposed repository additions taxStatementRepository.ts possibly financialEvidenceRepository.ts if the evidence layer becomes reusable Guardrails for this epic This epic touches financial and semi-regulatory data, so the guardrails should be stricter than average. TX1: No silent tax assumptions If a figure depends on jurisdiction-specific logic and the rule profile is missing, the line must be flagged REQUIRES_REVIEW, not silently computed. TX2: Every computed line needs provenance Each computed line must store: rule used, input values, calculation basis, source mode. TX3: Manual override must remain visible If a user overrides a computed figure, the original computed value must remain inspectable. TX4: Imported bank/official values are authoritative when configured For mortgage interest and official tax values, imported/manual official figures should take precedence over inferred approximations. TX5: No mixing actual and flat-rate maintenance deduction If the tax profile supports both methods, the statement must enforce one chosen method per property/year. TX6: Tax statement output is “tax-prep support,” not certified advice UI and export must clearly indicate: prepared from available data, subject to owner/accountant review, jurisdiction configuration dependent. TX7: Canonical include definitions apply Any DTO that returns tax statement detail must use centralized includes per G9. TX8: Contract tests required New endpoints must have contract tests asserting: line shape, evidence linkage, property classification, totals and status fields. Proposed implementation slices Slice 1 — Data model + statement shell Create the core statement object and basic CRUD/listing. Deliverables schema additions for tax statement entities routes for create/list/get/update owner/manager list page statement detail shell Acceptance criteria user can create a tax statement for a year/owner user can attach one or more properties statement persists status and review metadata no DTO/include drift Slice 2 — Property classification + official values capture Add property usage classification and input of official tax values / imputed rental values. Deliverables wizard step or detail form validation schemas support for official value entry optional source attachment linkage Acceptance criteria each property must be classified before statement can finalize owner-occupied and rented properties take different paths missing required official values block readiness and produce actionable issues Slice 3 — Income line generation Generate rental or imputed-rent lines per property. Deliverables tax line generator service lease/rent roll extraction for rental income imputed rental manual/imported/configured-calculation path property-level income section UI Acceptance criteria rented property generates rental-income line owner-occupied property generates imputed-rental path mixed-use property creates split/review issue instead of bad silent output Slice 4 — Deduction engine for mortgage interest and maintenance Add deductions with evidence mapping. Deliverables mortgage interest input + evidence expense classification into deductible buckets actual-vs-flat-rate method selection statement deduction section Acceptance criteria mortgage interest cannot be inferred from balance alone maintenance deduction source records are inspectable disallowed or unclassified expenses are visibly excluded or flagged no double counting across deduction methods Slice 5 — Reconciliation report Build the “salary certificate equivalent” inside the product. Deliverables reconciliation grid/report source-to-line mapping exportable appendix confidence / issue badges Acceptance criteria every material statement line has a visible source path exported report helps an accountant map numbers into a tax declaration manual entries are distinguishable from computed/imported lines Slice 6 — Export package Generate PDF/CSV package for owner/accountant review. Deliverables PDF summary CSV line export evidence checklist optional per-property appendix Acceptance criteria user can export statement package export includes assumptions and unresolved issues totals in export match UI totals exactly Future slices canton-specific rule packs OCR-assisted import of bank/tax authority attestations accountant review mode cross-year carry-forward comparisons foreign property expanded handling sale / capital gains / major renovation treatment Validation checklist Before merge of any slice: Backend schema drift check empty prisma generate tsc --noEmit canonical include constants for tax statement DTOs contract tests for new tax statement endpoints Frontend follows F-UI1/F-UI2 depending on page type no inline styles repeated stateful tax UI extracted into shared components if reused empty states and review banners use global component classes Financial correctness totals = sum of constituent lines override state is preserved excluded lines are visible, not dropped source mapping exists for all material statement lines Risks R1. Canton specificity Swiss tax treatment varies. Hard-coding one-size-fits-all logic will create false confidence. Mitigation: rule profiles + review-required status when incomplete. R2. Source incompleteness The platform may not hold all necessary external tax data. Mitigation: first-class manual entry and evidence attachment, not fake automation. R3. Expense classification ambiguity Maintenance vs improvement may not always be derivable from invoice data. Mitigation: classification workflow + review queue + manual override. R4. Overlap with finance/reporting This epic can sprawl into full accounting. Mitigation: keep scope to annual tax-prep statement generation. Definition of done This epic is done when: An owner or manager can create an annual tax statement in the tool. Properties can be classified by tax treatment. The system generates the key wealth, income, and deduction lines. Every material line has visible provenance. The user can export a reviewable statement package. Missing legal/jurisdiction data results in explicit review flags, not silent assumptions. Contract tests and financial correctness tests protect the new surface. Suggested epic-history narrative Delivered a Swiss Property Tax Statement Journey for owners and managers, turning operational real-estate data into a tax-preparation workflow inside the platform. Added annual tax statement entities, property usage classification, line generation for tax value, rental income or imputed rental value, mortgage interest, and maintenance deductions, plus a reconciliation layer that maps each figure to its evidence and calculation rule. The journey does not file taxes directly; instead it produces a jurisdiction-aware, reviewable statement package that gives owners and accountants confidence similar to a salary certificate workflow, without hiding missing data or hard-coding brittle legal assumptions. Ticket: API-03 — Document implemented owner routes in OpenAPI

Goal

Close the final open audit finding by bringing apps/api/openapi.yaml into sync with the implemented owner-facing routes. Ensure the spec matches the real backend contract so client generation, contract tests, and future integrations do not silently drift.

Why this ticket

Project state says the remaining open audit finding is:

API-03: owner routes missing in openapi.yaml

The platform is now materially larger after FIN-COA and FIN-LEDGER. Spec accuracy matters more now because finance and owner features are exposed across multiple clients and proxies.

In scope
Add missing owner routes to apps/api/openapi.yaml
Add any missing schemas referenced by those routes
Add missing query/path/body parameter definitions
Update packages/api-client if required
Add or update tests that verify spec coverage for those routes
Out of scope
Runtime behavior changes
Owner UI redesign
New owner features
Auth model changes
Refactoring unrelated OpenAPI sections
Files to inspect first
PROJECT_STATE.md
docs/AUDIT.md
apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md
apps/api/openapi.yaml
apps/api/src/server.ts
owner-related backend route modules, likely including:
apps/api/src/routes/invoices.ts
apps/api/src/routes/leases.ts
apps/api/src/routes/financials.ts
any route modules registering /owner/* or owner-usable endpoints
packages/api-client/src/index.ts
existing OpenAPI / contract tests under apps/api/src/__tests__/
Implementation notes
Treat backend routes as source of truth, not the current spec.
Do not invent endpoints; only spec implemented routes.
Preserve existing response envelopes.
If a route returns DTOs already defined elsewhere, reuse existing schemas rather than duplicating.
If an owner route uses the same handler as manager/owner shared endpoints, ensure the documented auth/summary matches actual behavior.
Keep changes localized.
Acceptance criteria
All currently implemented owner-facing routes are represented in apps/api/openapi.yaml
Missing request/response schemas are added
packages/api-client is updated if needed
OpenAPI sync / contract tests pass for the newly documented routes
No runtime route behavior changes
Audit finding API-03 can be marked resolved
Test protocol
Run backend type check
Run OpenAPI sync / contract tests
Run targeted route tests for affected owner endpoints
Regenerate any api-client artifacts if that is part of repo workflow
Confirm no diff beyond intended spec/client/test updates
Validation checklist
Routes remain thin
No Prisma changes
No schema drift risk introduced
Spec matches actual route behavior
Response envelopes remain consistent
Tests updated with the spec change
Post-validation updates
Mark API-03 resolved in docs/AUDIT.md
Update PROJECT_STATE.md open-audit count
Refresh roadmap/planning state
Recalculate next ticket
4. Tests added/updated

Recommended test updates for this ticket:

Update the existing OpenAPI sync test to include the missing owner routes.
Add or extend contract assertions for the newly documented owner endpoints.
If there is a KNOWN_UNSPECCED_ROUTES list, remove the owner-route entries and assert the list stays empty for those paths.

Suggested categories:

Spec coverage test
Route-to-spec parity test
api-client surface test if client methods are generated or maintained manually
5. Validation summary

Expected validation outcome for this ticket:

No database changes
No migration required
No runtime behavior changes
OpenAPI becomes consistent with implemented owner routes
Final named audit gap closes cleanly
6. Context/guiding files refreshed

After completion, refresh:

docs/AUDIT.md
PROJECT_STATE.md
apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md if route counts or file index references change
blueprint.js / blueprint-generated derived counts if your workflow expects it
roadmap/planning state for ticket completion

TC-11 — Shared test server lifecycle helpers
Goal

Eliminate cross-suite integration test instability by extracting a single shared startServer / stopServer test helper and migrating server-spawning suites to use it consistently.

Why this ticket

Project state still calls out this technical debt:

copied startServer logic exists across many test files
orphaned handles corrupt later suites
some suites fail in full serial runs but pass individually

This is now the highest-leverage reliability ticket because:

Type: INFRA / TEST HARDENING
Priority: High

Problem statement

Integration tests currently duplicate backend bootstrapping logic across many files. That duplication causes subtle differences in:

port usage
teardown behavior
environment setup
async shutdown timing

Result:

failures are harder to diagnose because each file owns its own lifecycle logic
Outcome

Create one canonical test helper for API integration suites and migrate server-based tests to use it, so serial full-suite runs behave the same as single-suite runs.

In scope

Ensure teardown closes server handles cleanly

Out of scope

Replacing Jest
Changing production server bootstrap architecture
Broad test data/fixture redesign
Non-test refactors unrelated to lifecycle stability
Architecture fit

affects only test infrastructure
preserves runtime behavior
aligns with hardening goals already documented in CONTRIBUTING.md

Create a shared helper with a small, explicit API, for example:

stopTestServer(server)
optional helpers for:

2. Normalize teardown

Primary workflow affected:

invoice issue workflow
invoice payment workflow
other finance workflows only if currently missing canonical posting coverage

Files to modify — in this order:

apps/api/src/services/ledgerService.ts
affected workflows
repositories only if additional source relations must be loaded canonically
DTO / OpenAPI / api-client only if a contract changes
tests
finance UI only if a very small explanatory note is needed

Auth:
no auth changes

In scope:

Audit and harden automatic posting coverage for:
rent invoice issued
rent invoice paid
expense invoice issued
expense invoice paid
Ensure correct account selection based on existing COA / mapping rules
Ensure correct building/unit/source attribution where available
Add or improve tests proving debit/credit correctness and source linkage

Out of scope:

No manual journal entry UI
No new accounting regime
No redesign of invoice lifecycle
No budget/forecast features
No general close / period-locking functionality

Implementation requirements:

Preserve current best-effort/non-blocking posting semantics only if intentionally part of the current design.
Keep posting logic centralized in ledger service helpers and workflow orchestration.
Prefer adding golden/business-correctness tests over ad-hoc spot checks.
Be explicit in code/tests about whether amounts are accrual-based and when posting occurs.

Required output before code:

Current posting coverage found
Gaps found in invoice/expense posting coverage
Whether schema/API changes are needed or workflow/service-only changes are sufficient
Files to change
Any relevant open audit items
Short implementation plan

Definition of done:

npx tsc --noEmit — 0 errors
relevant ledger/invoice tests pass
npm run blueprint — docs sync cleanly
invoice issue/payment events are journaled consistently and test-covered
account selection and entity attribution are correct according to current COA/mapping rules
no posting logic moved into routes or UI
Recommended order

I’d run these prompts in this order:

1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6 → 7

Why:

1 gives you the backend read models
2 and 3 make finance actually usable
4 and 5 clean up the advanced accounting UI
6 strengthens the dimensional model
7 hardens trust in the accounting engine
My recommendation on scope

If you want the fastest path to “finance feels actionable,” do Prompts 1–3 first before touching deeper ledger correctness work.

That gives you:

portfolio overview
building overview
usable finance entry point

And only then improve the advanced accounting layer.

I can also turn these into a roadmap-ready DT ticket series with IDs, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and test protocols formatted like your existing tickets.

intake_items.title: Automate rent invoices generation
intake_items.raw_text: When a lease is signed, we should be able to look into the lease to grab the rent, the charges, and compute an invoice and submit it automatically to the tenant every end of the month for the next month, e.g. the rent for a month m is due at latest on m/d-1. For the invoice to be visible to the tenant, we should always send on the first day of current month the invoice for m+1 with a payment maturity on the last day of m. so for example if the tenant has signed a lease that starts on april 1st, the rent is due latest on the last day of march. the invoice for may should go out to the tenant as early as the 1st of april and is payable on the last day of april. etc. Invoices should contain clear lines that separate net rent from charges, and the last line should be the gross due rent in bold.

## Ticket DT-027: Filter rental docs page to selected units

**Goal:** Only show selected units on the supporting documents page.
**Phase:** P0 | **Area:** tenant-portal | **Shape:** UI-only

### Steps
1. Read apps/web/pages/tenant/apply/[applicationId]/documents.js to understand:
   - How the application object is fetched and what field holds the selected units
   - How the unit list is rendered (mapped array, grouped by unit, etc.)

2. After loading, derive the filtered set:
   If the application has application.units (array of objects):
   const selectedUnitIds = new Set(application.units.map(u => u.id));
   const displayUnits = allUnits.filter(u => selectedUnitIds.has(u.id));

   If the application has application.unitIds (array of strings):
   const displayUnits = allUnits.filter(u => application.unitIds.includes(u.id));

3. Render displayUnits instead of allUnits.

4. If displayUnits.length === 1, skip the unit heading/grouping label — render the
   document upload fields directly without a unit section header.
